role: Progressive cleric; member of the Assembly of Experts for the Constitution
bio: Ali Golzadeh Ghafouri was a progressive Shia cleric who was elected to the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979 as an independent from Tehran Province with over 1.5 million votes. He opposed the doctrine of velayat-e faqih and the concentration of power in the hands of a Supreme Leader. After the consolidation of clerical power, he withdrew from politics in 1981. His family suffered severe repression: two of his sons were executed in 1981, and his daughter and son-in-law were executed during the [1988 mass executions](/events/mass-executions-1988).
editorial_notes: Born 1923, died 2010. One of the highest vote-getters in Tehran in the 1979 assembly election (1,560,970 votes). Withdrew from politics after 1981 crackdown.
